message:
fix(tools): UTF-8 safe subject truncation + mail status/lock diagnostics tests

- Fix subject truncation in send_message and reply_message to use
  char-boundary-safe iteration instead of byte slicing, which would
  panic on multi-byte UTF-8 characters (br-2ei.20)
- Add handle_mail_with_conn testable variant and 2 integration tests
  for mail status command (shows counts, empty project)
- Add 4 unit tests for collect_lock_status (nonexistent root, empty
  root, finds lock files, includes owner metadata)
- Promote 3 features from Implemented to Verified (Tests) in
  FEATURE_PARITY.md: subject truncation, lock status diagnostics,
  mail status

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-2ei.2.6","title":"Archive: read helpers + commit metadata (mailbox-with-commits/outbox/inbox views)","description":"Implement archive reads used by resources that include commit context.

Scope:
- Given a canonical message path, map to git commit that introduced it.
- Provide lightweight commit metadata (sha, summary, authored_ts) for mailbox/outbox views.
- Support `whois(include_recent_commits=true)` by reading recent archive commits filtered by author.

Implementation:
- Prefer git2 for log traversal.
- Cache where sensible to avoid O(N) per request.

Acceptance:
- Resource outputs match legacy Python shapes for mailbox-with-commits and whois.
- Benchmarks cover common read paths.

 {"id":"br-2ei.2.7","title":"Reservations: implement force_release_file_reservation (inactivity heuristics + optional notify)","description":"Implement force-release stale file reservations (MCP tool).

Spec:
- Validate reservation appears abandoned:
  - agent inactive beyond threshold
  - no recent mail activity
  - no recent filesystem/git activity (archive)
- When released, optionally notify previous holder with a human-readable note.

Implementation plan:
- Define which activity signals we can reliably measure in Rust:
  - `agent.last_active_ts` in DB
  - last message activity timestamps (inbox/outbox/canonical)
  - archive activity timestamps (last commit touching agent/mail artifacts)
- Implement heuristics with conservative defaults and an explicit “why” explanation string.
- Write release to DB + archive artifacts (so humans can audit forced releases).
- If `notify_previous=true`: send a threaded message to the previous holder explaining the forced release and including the heuristics snapshot.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Safety / correctness:
  - Force-release is denied unless heuristics strongly indicate abandonment (tests cover deny path).
  - When allowed, release updates both DB state and archive artifacts (tests assert both).
  - No-op behavior when reservation already released/expired is well-defined.
- Observability:
  - Tool output includes a machine-parseable explanation (what signals were checked + thresholds + timestamps used).
  - Optional notify message includes the explanation and the operator’s note.
- Conformance:
  - Fixture-based conformance tests cover at least: allow path, deny path, and allow-with-notify path.
- Tests:
  - Unit tests cover heuristic evaluation boundary conditions (just-below/just-above thresholds).
  - Integration test runs through the full tool pipeline and validates resulting archive messages + reservation state.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:10:29.903046515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:30:28.285447850Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:30:28.285367769Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.7","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.7","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.7","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.2.8","title":"Archive spec: exact paths/frontmatter/commit + .gitattributes + attachment policy","description":"Extract the exact legacy git-archive behavior into a self-contained spec + test vectors.

Must extract verbatim from legacy Python:
- Archive root path rules (defaults + env/CLI overrides)
- Repo initialization behavior:
  - exact directory layout
  - exact .gitattributes content (line endings + binary rules)
  - initial commit behavior + message (if any)
- Message path format rules:
  - timestamp formatting
  - subject slug rules (unicode, punctuation, truncation)
  - ID formatting
- Frontmatter format:
  - exact header line(s)
  - JSON formatting rules (pretty vs compact, key ordering if any)
  - trailing newlines
- Outbox/inbox copy rules
- Commit batching semantics (what gets grouped into a commit, commit message format)
- Reservation artifact JSON schema and naming (sha1 inputs, casing)
- Attachment policy:
  - hashing algorithm(s)
  - WebP conversion policy triggers
  - inline thresholds and exact markdown embedding format
  - manifest/audit log formats

Deliverables:
- Explicit spec section in EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md (or equivalent) with concrete examples.
- Test vectors for slug/frontmatter and a golden file tree for 1-2 representative messages.

Acceptance:
- Implementers can build br-2ei.2.* without consulting Python again.
- Unit tests can assert byte-for-byte markdown + JSON artifact equality.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:54:29.422073298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:59:38.804611487Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:59:38.804548929Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.8","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:54:29.422073298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-2ei.3","title":"Guard: pre-commit/pre-push enforcement + install/uninstall tooling","description":"Implement the Agent Mail guard system (pre-commit / pre-push hooks) that prevents edits when exclusive file reservations conflict.

Spec source:
- EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md section "Guard Behavior (Pre-Commit / Pre-Push)"

Scope:
- `mcp-agent-mail-guard` crate:
  - install_guard(project, repo): resolves hooks dir (core.hooksPath or default), writes chain-runner hooks, writes plugin scripts under hooks.d/<hook>.
  - uninstall_guard(repo): removes our plugins; removes chain-runner only if safe (no other plugins) and restores .orig if applicable.
  - guard_status(repo): reports resolved hooks path + presence of pre-commit/pre-push + mode.
  - guard_check(repo, paths, advisory): checks active file reservations and returns conflicts (pattern, holder, expires_ts).
- MCP tools:
  - install_precommit_guard(project_key, code_repo_path)
  - uninstall_precommit_guard(code_repo_path)
- CLI wiring:
  - `am guard install|uninstall|status|check` delegates to the guard crate.

Implementation notes:
- Use git2 to discover repo + read config.
- Path matching: prefer pathspec (gitignore syntax) with symmetric fnmatch fallback.
- Must respect env vars: AGENT_MAIL_GUARD_MODE, AGENT_MAIL_BYPASS, AGENT_NAME.

## Success Criteria
1. Guard tool outputs match legacy fixtures (install/uninstall/status/check).
2. Unit + integration tests cover: hooksPath/worktrees, idempotent install/uninstall, path matching, rename handling, conflict detection.
3. E2E guard suite (br-2ei.9.3) blocks commits on conflicts and allows after release, with clear stderr and rich artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:06:34.458664027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:32:18.466527207Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:32:18.466466844Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.3.1","title":"Guard: resolve repo + hooks dir (core.hooksPath, worktrees) via git2","description":"Implement robust hooks directory resolution.

Requirements:
- Input: repo working tree path (code_repo_path).
- Discover git repo (supports worktrees).
- Determine hooks dir:
  - If core.hooksPath is set: resolve relative to repo root per git semantics.
  - Else: use default hooks dir for the repository common dir.
- Return resolved hooks_dir as a real filesystem path.

Must handle:
- .git is a file containing gitdir: ...
- worktrees where hooks live in common dir
- bare repo (if encountered) -> treat as invalid for hook install

Deliverable:
- `mcp_agent_mail_guard::resolve_hooks_dir(repo: &Path) -> GuardResult<PathBuf>` (or equivalent internal helper) with unit tests.
","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:07:47.277296976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:18:05.735479435Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:18:05.735461692Z","close_reason":"Implemented resolve_hooks_dir via git2 + commondir parsing; added unit tests incl. worktree case.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.3.2","title":"Guard: install/uninstall chain-runner hooks + agent-mail plugin scripts","description":"Implement install/uninstall mechanics (pre-commit and optionally pre-push).

Install behavior (match legacy):
- Ensure hooks.d/<hook>/ directory exists.
- Write/overwrite top-level hook script (chain-runner) that executes hooks.d/<hook>/* in lexical order.
- Preserve existing non-chain hook as <hook>.orig.
- Install our plugin under hooks.d/<hook>/50-agent-mail (or equivalent) that executes the actual guard check.
- Create Windows shims (.cmd/.ps1) if needed to invoke the chain-runner.

Uninstall behavior:
- Remove our plugins (hooks.d/<hook>/50-agent-mail.*).
- Only remove chain-runner if it is ours AND there are no other plugins remaining AND no .orig restoration is needed.
- If .orig exists and chain-runner is removed, restore it.

Deliverables:
- `install_guard(project_key, repo)` writes files and chmods +x on unix.
- `uninstall_guard(repo)` removes/restores safely.
- Unit tests use temp git repos.
